content_en:
Shams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Uthman adh-Dhahabi was born in 673 AH (1274 CE) in Damascus or its environs, into a family of Turkish origin that had settled in Syria. He became the most prolific and authoritative hadith critic of the Mamluk era and one of the greatest biographers and historians in the Islamic scholarly tradition. His works on hadith criticism, biographical dictionaries, and Islamic history remain indispensable references.

Adh-Dhahabi studied under an extraordinary array of teachers, including major figures in hadith scholarship, history, and Qur'anic sciences. He studied in Damascus and traveled to Egypt, the Hijaz, and other centers of learning. Among his teachers were scholars of the caliber of Dimyati, Ibn Daqiq al-Eid, and Ibn Taymiyyah — though he maintained his independence from Ibn Taymiyyah's more controversial theological positions while admiring his scholarly breadth.

His career was marked by extraordinary productivity. Among his major works are Mizan al-I'tidal (a critical dictionary of hadith transmitters), Tazkirat al-Huffaz (biographies of major hadith scholars), Tarikh al-Islam (a comprehensive history of Islam), and Al-Kashif (a condensed hadith transmitter dictionary). The Siyar A'lam an-Nubala' — Lives of Noble Figures — is his most expansive biographical work and arguably his most enduring contribution.

The Siyar was composed in the mature period of adh-Dhahabi's career, drawing on his encyclopedic knowledge of the biographical literature and his own critical judgment developed over decades of hadith scholarship. The Mamluk period when he wrote was characterized by intense scholarly activity in Damascus and Cairo despite the political disruptions of the era, including Mongol raids and crusading activity in the Levant. The scholarly establishment maintained its traditions with remarkable resilience, and adh-Dhahabi was its most prolific representative. He died in Damascus in 748 AH (1348 CE). His collected works constitute the most comprehensive individual contribution to Islamic biographical and historical scholarship in the tradition, and the Siyar stands as the capstone of this achievement — a work so vast in scope, so rigorous in method, and so sound in judgment that later biographical scholars consistently positioned their own works in dialogue with it rather than as replacements for it. Students who learn to use the Siyar efficiently gain access to one of the most powerful research tools available for the study of classical Islamic civilization.
content_id:
Syams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Utsman adz-Dzahabi lahir pada tahun 673 H (1274 M) di Damaskus atau sekitarnya, dalam keluarga berketurunan Turki yang telah menetap di Suriah. Ia menjadi kritikus hadis paling produktif dan otoritatif pada era Mamluk serta salah satu biograf dan sejarawan terbesar dalam tradisi keilmuan Islam. Karya-karyanya tentang kritik hadis, kamus-kamus biografi, dan sejarah Islam tetap menjadi referensi yang tak tergantikan.

Adz-Dzahabi belajar kepada sejumlah guru yang luar biasa, termasuk tokoh-tokoh utama dalam ilmu hadis, sejarah, dan ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran. Ia belajar di Damaskus dan bepergian ke Mesir, Hijaz, dan pusat-pusat pembelajaran lainnya. Di antara guru-gurunya adalah ulama-ulama sekaliber ad-Dimyati, Ibn Daqiq al-Id, dan Ibn Taimiyah — meskipun ia mempertahankan independensinya dari posisi-posisi teologis Ibn Taimiyah yang lebih kontroversial sambil mengagumi keluasan ilmiahnya.

Kariernya ditandai dengan produktivitas yang luar biasa. Di antara karya-karya utamanya adalah Mizan al-I'tidal (kamus kritis perawi-perawi hadis), Tadzkirat al-Huffadz (biografi ulama-ulama hadis utama), Tarikh al-Islam (sejarah Islam yang komprehensif), dan Al-Kasyif (kamus perawi hadis yang diringkas). Siyar A'lam an-Nubala — Kehidupan Tokoh-tokoh Mulia — adalah karya biografi paling luas miliknya dan bisa dikatakan kontribusinya yang paling abadi.

Siyar disusun pada masa matang karier adz-Dzahabi, dengan mengandalkan pengetahuan ensiklopedisnya tentang literatur biografi dan penilaian kritisnya sendiri yang dikembangkan selama beberapa dekade ilmu hadis. Periode Mamluk ketika ia menulis ditandai dengan aktivitas ilmiah yang intens di Damaskus dan Kairo meskipun ada gangguan-gangguan politik pada era itu, termasuk serangan Mongol dan aktivitas Perang Salib di Levant. Lembaga ilmiah mempertahankan tradisinya dengan ketahanan yang luar biasa, dan adz-Dzahabi adalah perwakilannya yang paling produktif. Ia wafat di Damaskus pada tahun 748 H (1348 M). Karya-karyanya yang terkumpul merupakan kontribusi individual paling komprehensif terhadap ilmu biografi dan sejarah Islam dalam tradisi ini, dan Siyar berdiri sebagai puncak pencapaian ini — sebuah karya yang begitu luas cakupannya, begitu ketat metodenya, dan begitu tepat penilaiannya sehingga para biograf kemudian secara konsisten memposisikan karya-karya mereka sendiri dalam dialog dengannya daripada sebagai penggantinya. Para mahasiswa yang belajar menggunakan Siyar secara efisien mendapatkan akses ke salah satu alat penelitian paling kuat yang tersedia untuk mempelajari peradaban Islam klasik.
content_ar: Shams ad-Din محمد ﷺ ibn أحمد ibn عثمان adh-Dhahabi was born in 673 AH (1274 CE) in Damascus or its environs, into a الأسرة of Turkish origin that had settled in Syria. He became the most prolific and المعتمد الحديث critic of the Mamluk era and one of the greatest biographers and historians in the Islamic العلمي التراث. His works on الحديث criticism, biographical dictionaries, and Islamic history remain indispensable references. Adh-Dhahabi studied under an extraordinary array of teachers, بما في ذلك major figures in الحديث scholarship, history, and Qur'anic sciences. He studied in Damascus and traveled to Egypt, the Hijaz, and other centers of learning. Among his teachers were العلماء of the caliber of Dimyati, Ibn Daqiq al-Eid, and ابن تيمية — though he maintained his independence from ابن تيمية's more controversial theological positions بينما admiring his العلمي breadth. His career was marked by extraordinary productivity. Among his major works are الميزان al-I'tidal (a البالغ الأهمية dictionary of الحديث transmitters), Tazkirat al-Huffaz (biographies of major الحديث العلماء), Tarikh al-الإسلام (a comprehensive history of الإسلام), and Al-Kashif (a condensed الحديث transmitter dictionary). The Siyar A'lam an-Nubala' — Lives of Noble Figures — is his most expansive biographical work and arguably his most enduring contribution. The Siyar was composed in the mature period of adh-Dhahabi's career, drawing on his encyclopedic العلم of the biographical literature and his own البالغ الأهمية judgment developed over decades of الحديث scholarship. The Mamluk period عندما كتب was characterized by intense العلمي activity in Damascus and Cairo رغم the political disruptions of the era, بما في ذلك Mongol raids and crusading activity in the Levant. The العلمي establishment maintained its traditions with remarkable resilience, and adh-Dhahabi was its most prolific representative. He died in Damascus in 748 AH (1348 CE). His collected works constitute the most comprehensive individual contribution to Islamic biographical and historical scholarship in the التراث, and the Siyar stands as the capstone of this achievement — a work لذا vast in scope, لذا rigorous in method, and لذا sound in judgment that later biographical العلماء consistently positioned their own works in dialogue with it rather than as replacements for it. Students who learn to use the Siyar efficiently gain access to one of the most powerful research tools available for the study of الكلاسيكي Islamic civilization.
